//: OPT-IN prompt/agent-type hooks (LLM-as-judge / guardrail). These cost an LLM call PER event, so they are NOT enabled by default. To use: merge into the plugin's hooks/hooks.json (plugin-level) or a consuming repo's .claude/settings.json. Pick the ones worth the cost on your project.
//prompt-judge: prompt-type Stop hook — a cheap LLM-as-judge on the turn's outcome. $ARGUMENTS receives the hook stdin (assistant_message etc).
//agent-guardrail: agent-type UserPromptSubmit hook — delegates to the security-guardrail agent for injection/safety judgement the regex prompt-guard.py can't do.

Stop.prompt:
You are a delivery-quality judge. Given the turn: $ARGUMENTS
If the change touched code but skipped docs/tests, or looks risky, flag it. Reply with ONLY JSON: {"quality": "good"|"needs-revision", "issues": ["..."], "reason": "<one sentence>"}.
